proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Sex (sex_female) is associated with pfs_months.
Right-sided primary tumor (right_sided_primary=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0/1/2 mean PFS = 5.27/4.05/2.91 months. OLS slope -1.190 months per ECOG level.
Stage IV mean PFS 3.70 vs non-IV 5.06; difference -1.35 months.
Age coefficient is POSITIVE: +0.176 months per year of age (opposite of usual clinical expectation).
Female vs male PFS difference -0.006 months; not significant.
Right-sided vs left-sided PFS difference -0.305 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

Other routine labs (hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) have detectable univariable associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Albumin slope +0.465 months per g/dL — strong positive prognostic association.
Weight-loss slope -0.073 months per percentage point of 6-month weight loss.
CEA slope -0.00535 months per ng/mL.
LDH slope -0.00032 months per U/L (very small but statistically significant).
CRP slope -0.00202 months per mg/L; borderline non-significant.
NLR slope +0.00522 (wrong sign, non-significant).

Among routine labs: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (-0.00059, p=0.004) and total_bilirubin_mg_dl (-0.065, p=0.044) reach p<0.05 with directionally adverse signs; hemoglobin_g_dl (-0.010, p=0.078), creatinine_mg_dl (-0.046, p=0.18), AST/ALT/BUN/Na/K/Ca all non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with different pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.
msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.

Each targeted/cytotoxic therapy variable (treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib, treatment_regorafenib) has a non-zero unadjusted main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS-mutant patients have 0.327 months shorter PFS.
BRAF V600E patients have 0.228 months shorter PFS.
NRAS-mutant patients have 0.221 months LONGER PFS (counter-intuitive sign).
MSI-high status has no detectable univariable effect on PFS (-0.019 months).
HER2-amplified patients have a small non-significant -0.056 months PFS difference.
NTRK-fusion patients (n=251) show no detectable PFS difference.

Of the 6 treatments, only treatment_regorafenib has a substantial main effect: +0.972 months (p<1e-300). cetuximab -0.038 (p=0.09), bevacizumab -0.019 (p=0.35), pembrolizumab +0.005 (p=0.86), encorafenib +0.005 (p=0.87), trastuzumab/tucatinib -0.044 (p=0.25) — all near zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with kras_mutation=0 (KRAS WT) but not in kras_mutation=1, i.e. a positive cetuximab×KRAS-WT interaction (negative cetuximab:kras_mutation coefficient).
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with braf_v600e=0 (BRAF WT) but not in braf_v600e=1.
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with nras_mutation=0 (NRAS WT) but not in nras_mutation=1.
treatment_cetuximab benefit is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in msi_high=1 patients but not in msi_high=0.
treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months in braf_v600e=1 patients but not in braf_v600e=0.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months in her2_amplified=1 patients but not in her2_amplified=0.

analyses.result_summary: cetux:KRAS interaction +0.020 months, p=0.66; cetuximab main effect in KRAS WT only -0.048 months. No KRAS-driven cetuximab benefit.
cetux:BRAF interaction -0.026 months, p=0.81. Wrong direction for benefit in BRAF WT and not significant.
cetux:NRAS interaction -0.007 months, p=0.96.

analyses.code: Subgroup mean: cetux in left-sided RAS+BRAF WT n=16443, on=4.617 vs off=4.684
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab effect in canonical 'left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type' subgroup is -0.067 months (no benefit). Interaction cetux x left_rasbrafwt = -0.060, p=0.15.
pembro:MSI interaction +0.002, p=0.99. In MSI-high subgroup pembro on=4.299 vs off=4.292 (diff +0.007). No effect.
enco:BRAF interaction -0.141, p=0.38 (wrong direction). In BRAF V600E subgroup enco on=3.979 vs off=4.108 (diff -0.13). No benefit.
T+T:HER2 interaction +0.063, p=0.78. In HER2-amplified subgroup T+T on=4.273 vs off=4.256. No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, age_years, sex_female, right_sided_primary, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, biomarkers), only treatment_regorafenib has a non-zero average pfs_months effect among the 6 treatments.

Regorafenib-treated patients are not meaningfully different from non-regorafenib patients on baseline ECOG, stage_iv, age_years, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, or biomarker frequencies (i.e. its main effect is not driven by baseline confounding).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ all 6 treatments + ECOG + stage_iv + age + sex + sidedness + albumin + weight_loss + CEA + LDH + CRP + NLR + Hb + 6 biomarkers', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300), cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab +0.000 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), T+T +0.026 (p=0.07). Only regorafenib retains a meaningful effect after adjustment.

analyses.code: Tabulate baseline features by regorafenib status
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib+ vs - patients have nearly identical means: ECOG 0.79 vs 0.80, stage_iv 0.55 vs 0.55, age 65.1 vs 65.0, albumin 3.80 vs 3.80, weight loss 3.88 vs 3.83, KRAS 0.42 vs 0.42, BRAF 0.047 vs 0.045, MSI 0.050 vs 0.050. Treatment assignment is essentially balanced — main effect is real, not confounded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each treatment, treatment×feature interactions (with each of the 27 candidate features one at a time, controlling for ECOG/stage/age/albumin/weight loss) reveal substantial heterogeneity. The only treatment with such heterogeneity is regorafenib.

analyses.code: Loop: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ tx*feat + base', df).fit() for each tx,feat
analyses.result_summary: regorafenib has highly significant interactions: x kras_mutation (-1.63, p<1e-300), x right_sided_primary (-1.46, p<1e-300), x cea_ng_ml (-0.026, p<1e-300), x braf_v600e (-0.99, p=8e-97), x nras_mutation (+0.89, p=5e-54), x albumin_g_dl (-0.085, p=2e-5). All interactions for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib have |p|>0.014 and trivial coefficients (~0.01-0.08 magnitude with no convincing pattern).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially longer pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 (KRAS WT) patients than in kras_mutation=1 patients.
treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially longer pfs_months in braf_v600e=0 (BRAF WT) patients than in braf_v600e=1 patients.
treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially longer pfs_months in right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) patients than in right-sided.
treatment_regorafenib effect is largest when KRAS WT, BRAF WT, and left-sided are jointly satisfied; the effect compounds across these predicates.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect in KRAS WT: +1.66 months (n=29102, on=5.77 off=4.12, p<1e-300). In KRAS MUT: +0.027 months (n=20898, p=0.45). Strong qualitative interaction.
Regorafenib effect in BRAF WT: +1.03 months (p<1e-300). In BRAF V600E: -0.16 months (n=2272, p=0.15). Effect concentrated in BRAF WT.
Regorafenib effect in left-sided: +1.48 months (n=32343, p<1e-300). In right-sided: +0.026 months (n=17657, p=0.52). Effect confined to left-sided.

analyses.code: Subgroup means in KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided + multivariable interaction
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS WT & BRAF WT subgroup (n=26830) regorafenib effect = +1.81 months. In KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided (n=17372): +2.76 months. The 3-way interaction model gives main rego coefficient +2.29 with rego×KRAS = -1.79, rego×BRAF = -1.77, rego×right-sided = -1.47, all p<1e-300. Each unfavorable predicate roughly halves the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even after restricting to canonical left-sided RAS WT BRAF WT subgroup and adjusting for confounders, treatment_cetuximab has zero pfs_months effect.
Even after restricting to MSI-high subgroup and adjusting for confounders, treatment_pembrolizumab has zero pfs_months effect.
Even after restricting to BRAF V600E subgroup and adjusting for confounders, treatment_encorafenib has zero pfs_months effect.
Even after restricting to HER2-amplified subgroup and adjusting for confounders, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has zero pfs_months effect.
Cetuximab + encorafenib combination produces a positive pfs_months effect in BRAF V600E patients beyond either single agent.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab + ECOG+stage+age+albumin+weight loss+CEA+regorafenib', df[left RAS+BRAF WT]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab adjusted coef in left-sided RAS+BRAF WT subgroup = -0.026 (p=0.16). No detectable benefit even in canonical subgroup.
Adjusted pembrolizumab coef in MSI-high (n=2513) = +0.0585 (p=0.26). No real benefit.
Adjusted encorafenib coef in BRAF V600E (n=2272) = -0.0058 (p=0.69). No benefit.
Adjusted T+T coef in HER2-amplified (n=1504) = +0.0041 (p=0.97). No benefit.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test: cetux+enco combo vs anything else in BRAF V600E
analyses.result_summary: Combo cetux+enco in BRAF V600E (n=58 of 2272) PFS 4.15 vs 4.09 in others (diff +0.058, p=0.83). No combo synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: T-learner with gradient boosting outcome models produces individual treatment-effect estimates whose largest leaves (split by depth-3 regression tree on baseline features) are defined by cea_ng_ml ≤ ~5 ng/mL together with kras_mutation=0 and right_sided_primary=0 for regorafenib, predicting tau_mean ≈ +4 months in that leaf.

T-learner CATE trees for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib reveal no leaf with mean tau exceeding ±0.4 months, consistent with no real heterogeneous treatment effect for these agents.

analyses.code: GradientBoostingRegressor T-learner; DecisionTreeRegressor max_depth=3 on tau
analyses.result_summary: Top regorafenib leaf: cea_ng_ml<=4.95 & kras_mutation==0 & right_sided_primary==0 → mean tau = +3.97 months (n=10056). Next leaves drop to +0.66, +0.47, +0.40, all defined by violations of the above triple. Cohort-mean tau = +0.93. The CATE-tree splits exactly recapitulate the directly-estimated subgroup definition.

analyses.code: Same T-learner pipeline for each of the other 5 treatments
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab: max leaf tau +0.054, min -0.009. Bevacizumab: max +0.063, min -0.009. Pembrolizumab: max +0.247 (only 755 patients, age 57.55-58.05). Encorafenib: max +0.128 (small). T+T: max +0.36 (n=501, age<45.55 or AST>66 — not biologically coherent). No coherent positive subgroup beyond noise for any of these 5 treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS WT BRAF WT left-sided patients, the treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit declines monotonically with increasing cea_ng_ml (continuous interaction).
A cea_ng_ml cut at ~5 ng/mL separates patients with full regorafenib benefit (~+5 months) from those with sharply diminished benefit.
Within the optimal subgroup (KRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided, low CEA), ecog_ps and stage_iv further modify the regorafenib effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * cea_ng_ml + base', df[KRAS+BRAF WT, left]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Continuous interaction coefficient = -0.064 months per ng/mL CEA (p<1e-300). Regorafenib benefit declines steadily with rising CEA.

analyses.code: Cutpoint scan of CEA in KRAS+BRAF WT left-sided
analyses.result_summary: Diff (regorafenib on - off) by CEA ceiling: ≤2 → +5.04, ≤3 → +5.03, ≤4 → +5.04, ≤5 → +5.01, ≤6 → +4.54, ≤7 → +4.13, ≤8 → +3.90, ≤10 → +3.61, ≤15 → +3.26, ≤20 → +3.07. Effect plateaus at ~+5.0 below CEA 5 and declines above.

analyses.code: Stratified means in KRAS+BRAF WT, left, CEA<=5
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect by ECOG: 0 → +4.87, 1 → +5.09, 2 → +4.81 (very stable). By stage: stage_iv=0 → +5.03, stage_iv=1 → +5.01. ECOG and stage do not modify the regorafenib benefit within the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is uniquely concentrated in the joint subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml ≤ 5 ng/mL; outside this subgroup the average effect is essentially zero.
Each of the four subgroup predicates is necessary: violating any single one (KRAS mutant alone, BRAF V600E alone, right-sided alone, or CEA>5 alone) eliminates the regorafenib benefit.
Within the favorable subgroup, the regorafenib benefit is not modulated by other treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab/tucatinib).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +4.98 months (p<1e-300). Treatment_regorafenib main effect outside subgroup = -0.004 (p=0.11). Subgroup contains 9338 patients (1905 regorafenib-treated). Inside: PFS on=9.10 vs off=4.09 (+5.01 months). Outside: on=4.15 vs off=4.12 (+0.024 months, p=0.36).

analyses.code: Drop-one tests on the four predicates
analyses.result_summary: Full subgroup diff = +5.01 months. Drop KRAS WT requirement (so include KRAS mutants too): diff = +2.85. Drop BRAF WT requirement: +4.62. Drop left-sided requirement: +3.30. Drop CEA<=5 requirement: +2.76. Single-predicate-violation subgroups (only that predicate bad) all show ~0 benefit: KRAS MUT only-bad +0.07 (p=0.26), BRAF V600E only-bad -0.17 (p=0.37), right-sided only-bad +0.11 (p=0.12), CEA>5 only-bad -0.002. NRAS mutation status does not matter (effect +5.24 in NRAS-mut subset of optimal vs +5.00 in NRAS-WT subset).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * other_tx + base', df[favorable]).fit() for each other tx
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, no other treatment significantly modifies regorafenib effect: rego x cetux p=0.66, x bevacizumab p=0.96, x pembrolizumab p=0.40, x encorafenib p=0.69, x T+T p=0.22. The regorafenib benefit is independent of co-administered agents.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months relative to no regorafenib in patients meeting ALL four predicates: kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type) AND braf_v600e=0 (BRAF V600E wild-type) AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided primary) AND cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL (low baseline CEA). All four predicates are required; violating any one drops the benefit to ~0.

FINAL: treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have no detectable pfs_months effect in this cohort, including in their canonical biomarker subgroups (left-sided RAS/BRAF WT, MSI-high, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified respectively).

FINAL prognostic summary: the strongest independent prognostic features for pfs_months are ECOG (-1.17/level), stage_iv (-1.36), albumin (+0.47/g/dL), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.08/%), age_years (+0.18/year, positive sign), right_sided_primary (-0.29), kras_mutation (-0.35), braf_v600e (-0.36), cea_ng_ml (-0.005/ng/mL) and ldh_u_l (-0.0004/U/L).

analyses.code: Final binary subgroup model & necessity tests
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: regorafenib×favorable_subgroup interaction = +4.98 months (p<1e-300). Estimated subgroup-restricted causal effect on PFS ≈ +5.0 months. Outside-subgroup effect statistically null (+0.024, p=0.36). Each of the four predicates is individually necessary (single-violation subgroups all show <±0.2 months effect).

All adjusted treatment coefficients for cetuximab (-0.011), bevacizumab (+0.003), pembrolizumab (+0.000), encorafenib (+0.006), T+T (+0.026) have |coef|<0.03 with p>=0.07. In canonical biomarker subgroups: cetux in left-sided RAS+BRAF WT -0.026 (p=0.16), pembro in MSI-high +0.059 (p=0.26), enco in BRAF V600E -0.006 (p=0.69), T+T in HER2 amplified +0.004 (p=0.97). No evidence of a real PFS effect for any of these 5 agents in this cohort.

analyses.code: Adjusted multivariable OLS pfs_months ~ all features
analyses.result_summary: All coefficients above are simultaneously estimated in the multivariable model with t-statistics |t|>18 (p<1e-50) for ECOG, stage_iv, age, albumin, weight_loss, right_sided_primary, KRAS, BRAF, CEA, LDH; sex, NLR, hemoglobin, MSI, HER2, NRAS, NTRK and remaining lab features show no independent prognostic effect after adjustment.
